steps.title: Spot the two competing perspectives
steps.content: The passage presents a feature that critics disagree about: the abrupt endings of the novels. After describing one critical viewpoint, the word But introduces a completely different take on the same endings. The passage is organized around showing these two opposing reactions.

steps.title: Identify the main purpose
steps.content:
Choice C states exactly what the passage does: describe a characteristic and present two different viewpoints on it.

One view says the endings are arbitrary flaws; the other says they're intentional—the series is meant to be read as one long book, not separate novels.

The other choices either import unsupported claims (A: complexity; D: entertainment value) or confuse a supporting analogy with the main argument (B).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
